title: Replace frequency-only categorical features with grouped cross-fitted target priors
summary: The current best code uses category frequencies, but public guidance on target encoding shows that cross-fitted target statistics can add much more signal if leakage is controlled.
rationale: The current best code uses category frequencies, but public guidance on target encoding shows that cross-fitted target statistics can add much more signal if leakage is controlled. CatBoost’s ordered categorical transforms are based on the same principle.
implementation_hint: Generate out-of-fold target encodings with shrinkage for `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Race_Compound`, `Compound_Stint`, and hierarchical combos like `Race_Compound_Stint`. Use race-grouped folds and Bayesian smoothing toward broader parents such as `Compound` or `Race_Compound`.
expected_effect: Likely stronger than raw frequencies for capturing driver/team/track strategy tendencies while preserving robustness on rare categories.
risk: Without strict group-wise OOF generation this will overfit badly; unseen-category fallback must be explicit.
